description: The home page shall display a 'Welcome to the-internet' heading, an 'Available Examples' subheading, and a vertical list of hyperlinks where each link's text matches its example name.
expected_result: The page displays 'Welcome to the-internet' heading, 'Available Examples' subheading, and a vertical list of hyperlinks where each link's text matches its example name (e.g., 'Checkboxes', 'File Upload', 'Typos').

description: Most example pages shall display a consistent footer section containing the text 'Powered by Elemental Selenium'.
steps: Scroll to the bottom of the page.
expected_result: The page displays a footer containing the text 'Powered by Elemental Selenium'.

description: The checkboxes page shall display at least two checkbox input controls, labeled 'checkbox 1' and 'checkbox 2' or equivalent nearby text.
expected_result: The page displays at least two checkbox input controls, labeled 'checkbox 1' and 'checkbox 2' or with equivalent nearby text.

description: The user shall be able to toggle checkbox states by clicking them, and the target checkbox shall toggle its checked/unchecked state according to browser default behavior.
expected_result: The target checkbox toggles its checked/unchecked state according to browser default behavior.

description: The login page shall display a 'Login Page' heading, an input field labeled 'Username', an input field labeled 'Password', and a 'Login' button.
expected_result: The page displays a 'Login Page' heading, an input field labeled 'Username', an input field labeled 'Password', and a 'Login' button.

description: The system shall authenticate the user as successful and navigate to a 'secure area' page (e.g., /secure) that indicates successful login when valid credentials ('tomsmith' / 'SuperSecretPassword!') are provided.
expected_result: The system navigates to a 'secure area' page (e.g., /secure) that indicates successful login, and no error message is displayed.

description: The system shall display an error message indicating wrong information and not navigate to the secure area page when invalid credentials are provided.
steps: Enter any username and/or password combination other than 'tomsmith' / 'SuperSecretPassword!'.
expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that the information is wrong, does not navigate to the secure area page, and the error message is visually associated with the login form.

description: The dropdown page shall display a descriptive heading 'Dropdown List' and a dropdown (select element) with a default prompt 'Please select an option' and at least 'Option 1' and 'Option 2' as selectable options.
expected_result: The page displays a heading 'Dropdown List', a dropdown with a default prompt 'Please select an option', and includes 'Option 1' and 'Option 2' as selectable options.

description: The user shall be able to change the dropdown selection, and the dropdown's visible selection shall update to show the chosen option.
expected_result: The dropdown's visible selection updates to show the chosen option.

description: The page shall display descriptive text explaining asynchronous element changes and at least one control (checkbox or input field) that may be dynamically enabled, disabled, added, or removed after user actions.
expected_result: The page displays descriptive text about asynchronous element changes and at least one control (e.g., checkbox, input field) subject to dynamic changes.

description: The page shall display a heading 'Dynamically Loaded Page Elements' and provide two example links or sections: 'Example 1: Element on page that is hidden' and 'Example 2: Element rendered after the fact'.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'Dynamically Loaded Page Elements' and two example links/sections: 'Example 1: Element on page that is hidden' and 'Example 2: Element rendered after the fact'.

description: The page shall display a heading 'File Uploader', a file input control, an 'Upload' button, and may present a drag-and-drop area for file selection.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'File Uploader', a file input control, an 'Upload' button, and optionally a drag-and-drop area.

description: The system shall accept a selected file and update the page to reflect that the file has been uploaded when the 'Upload' button is clicked.
steps: Select a file using the file input or drag-and-drop.
expected_result: The system accepts the selected file and the page updates to reflect that the file has been uploaded (e.g., confirmation text).

description: The page shall display a heading 'JavaScript Alerts', three clickable controls labeled 'Click for JS Alert', 'Click for JS Confirm', and 'Click for JS Prompt', and a 'Result:' label.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'JavaScript Alerts', three buttons: 'Click for JS Alert', 'Click for JS Confirm', 'Click for JS Prompt', and a 'Result:' label.

description: Clicking the 'Click for JS Alert' button shall trigger a browser-native JavaScript alert dialog.
steps: Click the 'Click for JS Alert' button.
expected_result: A JavaScript alert dialog is displayed by the browser.

description: Clicking 'Click for JS Confirm' shall display a JavaScript confirm dialog, and clicking 'Click for JS Prompt' shall display a JavaScript prompt dialog, with the 'Result:' area potentially updating.
expected_result: Clicking 'Click for JS Confirm' displays a JavaScript confirm dialog, and clicking 'Click for JS Prompt' displays a JavaScript prompt dialog, with the 'Result:' area potentially updating to reflect user actions.

description: The page shall include a region that visually suggests drag-and-drop functionality.
expected_result: The page displays a region visually suggesting drag-and-drop functionality.

description: The page shall display a heading 'Data Tables', describe 'Example 1' and 'Example 2', and each example shall render a table with specific column headers and rows.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'Data Tables', describes 'Example 1' and 'Example 2', and each example renders a table with 'Last Name', 'First Name', 'Due', 'Web Site', 'Action' column headers and at least the specified rows.

description: Each row in the data tables shall include an 'edit' and a 'delete' action in the 'Action' column.
steps: View any table row in Example 1 or Example 2.
expected_result: Each row includes an 'edit' and a 'delete' action in the 'Action' column.

description: The page shall display a heading 'Notification Message' and a notification message above the heading, describing an outcome such as 'Action successful' or 'Action unsuccessful, please try again'.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'Notification Message' and a notification message above the heading (e.g., 'Action successful').

description: Clicking the link labeled 'Click here to load a new message.' shall display a new notification message, potentially with different text.
steps: Click the link labeled 'Click here to load a new message.'.
expected_result: A new notification message is displayed, and its text may be different from the previous one.

description: An advertisement modal shall automatically display on page load when the user navigates to /entry_ad for the first time or when the ad is enabled, and it shall be clearly distinguishable from the background content.
steps: Navigate to /entry_ad (ensure ad is enabled or clear cookies).
expected_result: An advertisement (modal or overlay) is displayed on the page and is clearly distinguishable from the background content.

description: The ad shall be dismissed from view when 'Close' is clicked and shall not reappear on subsequent page loads as long as the setting remains active.
steps: Click the 'Close' control associated with the ad.
expected_result: The ad is dismissed from view after 'Close' is clicked and does not appear on subsequent page loads.

description: Clicking the link or control labeled 'click here' associated with 'To re-enable it, click here.' shall cause the ad to display again on subsequent visits to /entry_ad.
steps: Click the link labeled 'click here' to re-enable the ad.
expected_result: After re-enabling, the ad is displayed on page load during subsequent visits to /entry_ad.

description: The page shall display descriptive text explaining that a typo may appear randomly, and on some page loads, at least one word or punctuation shall be incorrect, while on other loads, the text may render without such a typo.
expected_result: The page displays descriptive text about random typos, and on some loads, a typo (e.g., 'won,t') is present, while on others, it is absent.

description: The page shall display a heading 'Add/Remove Elements' and a button labeled 'Add Element'.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'Add/Remove Elements' and a button labeled 'Add Element'.

description: Clicking the 'Add Element' button shall add a new button or UI element to the page, and the system shall allow multiple additions.
expected_result: A new button or UI element (e.g., 'Delete' button) is added to the page, and the system allows multiple additions.

description: Clicking the control associated with a dynamically added element shall remove it from the DOM and make it no longer visible.
steps: Click 'Add Element' to add an element.
Click the newly added element (e.g., 'Delete' button).
expected_result: The clicked element is removed from the DOM and is no longer visible.

description: The page shall display descriptive text explaining elements may disappear/reappear on each load, and on some loads, certain menu items or elements shall be visible, while on others, at least one of them shall not be present.
steps: Load the page multiple times (including refresh).
Observe the presence of menu items or elements.
expected_result: The page displays descriptive text about elements disappearing/reappearing, and specific menu items/elements are sometimes present and sometimes absent across loads.

description: The page shall display a heading 'Hovers' and at least three user images, each associated with hidden details (name: userX, 'View profile' link).
expected_result: The page displays a heading 'Hovers' and at least three user images, each associated with hidden details (e.g., 'name: user1', 'View profile' link).

description: Hovering over an image shall reveal the corresponding user's name and 'View profile' link, and these details may be hidden again when the mouse leaves the image area.
steps: Move the mouse pointer over an image.
Move the mouse pointer away from the image.
expected_result: While hovering, the user's name and 'View profile' link become visible, and when the mouse leaves, the details may become hidden again.

description: The page shall describe A/B testing or split testing as a way to test different versions of pages to determine which works best.
expected_result: The page displays text describing A/B testing or split testing.

description: The page content may vary to reflect different versions, and the heading 'No A/B Test' may be present in the inspected variant.
steps: Optionally refresh the page multiple times.
Observe the page content and heading.
expected_result: The page content may vary across loads, and the heading 'No A/B Test' is present in some variants.

description: The page shall load new text and images on each page refresh, demonstrating changing content.
expected_result: New text and images are loaded on each page refresh.

description: When `?with_content=static` is appended to the URL or the 'click here' link is used, some of the content shall become static.
expected_result: Some content on the page remains static across refreshes.

description: The page shall explain HTTP status codes and list standard codes (Success, Redirection, Client Error, Server Error) with a link to a complete list.
expected_result: The page explains HTTP status codes, lists standard codes (Success, Redirection, Client Error, Server Error), and provides a link to a complete list.

description: The page shall render a numeric input field labeled 'Number'.
expected_result: A numeric input field labeled 'Number' is displayed.

description: The numeric input field shall accept numeric entry according to browser default behavior (e.g., up/down arrows).
steps: Interact with the numeric input field using keyboard arrows or by typing numbers.
expected_result: The field accepts numeric entry, and up/down arrows change the numeric value.

description: The page shall show a horizontal slider control and an associated value indicator to its right.
expected_result: A horizontal slider control and an associated value indicator to its right are displayed.

description: When the slider is focused and moved (keyboard arrows or mouse drag), the value displayed to the right shall update accordingly.
steps: Move the slider using keyboard arrows or mouse drag.
expected_result: The value displayed to the right of the slider updates to reflect the slider's position.

description: The page shall display a box area where right-click triggers a custom context menu item called 'the-internet'.
steps: Right-click within the specified box area.
expected_result: A custom context menu appears, including an item called 'the-internet'.

description: Selecting the custom context menu item 'the-internet' shall trigger a JavaScript alert.
steps: Right-click within the specified box area.
Select the 'the-internet' custom context menu item.
expected_result: A JavaScript alert is triggered.

description: The page shall display a table with headers 'Lorem, Ipsum, Dolor, Sit, Amet, Diceret, Action' and multiple rows with numbered values (Iuvaret0, Iuvaret1, etc.) and 'edit delete' actions.
expected_result: A table is displayed with headers 'Lorem, Ipsum, Dolor, Sit, Amet, Diceret, Action', multiple rows with numbered values (e.g., Iuvaret0, Iuvaret1), and 'edit delete' actions.

description: The page shall describe that locators are intentionally difficult (unique IDs, no helpful locators, canvas element).
expected_result: The page displays text describing that locators are intentionally difficult.

description: A modal window encouraging user action (e.g., sign up) shall display when the mouse moves out of the viewport pane.
steps: Move the mouse cursor out of the browser viewport (e.g., towards the top of the browser window).
expected_result: A modal window encouraging user action (e.g., sign up) is displayed.

description: The exit intent modal shall include a 'Close' control that dismisses it.
steps: Click the 'Close' control within the modal.
expected_result: The modal window is dismissed.

description: The page shall display a JQuery UI menu demonstrating nested menu items controlled by hover/mouse actions.
steps: Observe the menu and interact with it using hover/mouse.
expected_result: A JQuery UI menu demonstrating nested menu items controlled by hover/mouse actions is displayed.

description: The descriptive text shall explain that visibility of elements is controlled by JQuery and may not be obvious from HTML alone.
expected_result: The page displays descriptive text explaining JQuery controls element visibility.

description: The page shall contain a JavaScript error triggered on the onload event.
expected_result: A JavaScript error is reported in the browser console on page load.

description: The page shall describe that this can be a problem for normal JavaScript injection techniques.
expected_result: The page displays text describing that the JS error can be problematic for injection techniques.

description: The page shall present a deeply nested DOM structure with headings 'No Siblings' and 'Siblings' and a large tabular layout with hundreds of cells marked by level and index (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, … 50.50).
expected_result: A deeply nested DOM structure with headings 'No Siblings' and 'Siblings' and a large tabular layout with hundreds of cells (e.g., 1.1, 50.50) is displayed.

description: The page shall describe that this layout is used to demonstrate rendering and test performance issues when DOMs are large.
expected_result: The page displays text describing the layout's purpose for demonstrating rendering and performance issues.

description: The page shall implement infinite scrolling such that scrolling down loads additional content blocks.
expected_result: Additional content blocks are loaded as the user scrolls down.

description: The page shall contain a link or text 'next page'.
expected_result: A link or text 'next page' is present on the page.

description: The page shall present a list of files as links under the heading 'File Downloader'.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'File Downloader' and a list of files presented as clickable links.

description: Clicking on any file name link shall initiate a download of the corresponding file via the browser.
steps: Click on any file name link.
expected_result: The browser initiates a download of the corresponding file.

description: The page shall display a form with an 'E-mail' field and a 'Retrieve password' button under heading 'Forgot Password'.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'Forgot Password', a form with an 'E-mail' field, and a 'Retrieve password' button.

description: Submitting the form shall attempt to retrieve a password for the given email.
steps: Enter an email address in the 'E-mail' field.
expected_result: The system attempts to retrieve a password for the provided email.

description: The page shall display heading 'Geolocation' and a button labeled 'Where am I?'.
expected_result: The page displays heading 'Geolocation' and a button labeled 'Where am I?'.

description: Clicking 'Where am I?' shall attempt to access the user’s current latitude and longitude and display them on the page.
steps: (If prompted by browser) Grant location permission.
Observe the page for displayed coordinates.
expected_result: The system attempts to access the user's current latitude and longitude, and they are displayed on the page.

description: The page shall show a floating menu that remains visible while the user scrolls through long text content.
expected_result: A menu remains visible (floats) while the user scrolls through long text content.

description: The main content shall consist of multiple long paragraphs of placeholder Latin text.
expected_result: The main content consists of multiple long paragraphs of placeholder Latin text.

description: The page shall demonstrate shadow DOM usage, including the text 'My default text' and repeated lines like 'Let’s have some different text! In a list!'.
expected_result: The page displays text 'My default text' and repeated lines like 'Let’s have some different text! In a list!', demonstrating shadow DOM usage.

description: Some content shall be enclosed in shadow DOM such that direct DOM queries may not see it in the regular document tree.
steps: Attempt to query for specific content (e.g., 'My default text') using standard DOM queries in browser developer tools.
expected_result: Some content is enclosed in shadow DOM, and direct DOM queries may not find this content in the regular document tree.

description: The /frames page shall display a list of frame-related examples, including 'Nested Frames' and 'iFrame'.
expected_result: The page displays a list of frame-related examples, including 'Nested Frames' and 'iFrame'.

description: Selecting 'Nested Frames' shall navigate to /nested_frames where multiple frames (top/bottom, left/right) are embedded.
expected_result: The system navigates to /nested_frames, where multiple frames (top/bottom, left/right) are embedded.

description: Selecting 'iFrame' shall navigate to a page with an editable rich text area.
expected_result: The system navigates to a page with an editable rich text area.

description: The /windows page shall display text 'Opening a new window' and a link 'Click Here', and clicking the link shall open a new browser window or tab.
expected_result: The page displays text 'Opening a new window' and a 'Click Here' link, and a new browser window or tab opens.

description: The /multiple_windows page shall provide a similar multi-window demonstration.
steps: Click any link intended to open a new window/tab.
expected_result: The page demonstrates multi-window behavior, and a new browser window or tab opens.

description: The page shall list three examples: 'Menu Element', 'An image', 'List', describing that elements shift a few pixels on each page load.
expected_result: The page lists 'Menu Element', 'An image', 'List' examples and describes that elements shift a few pixels on each page load.

description: On reload, elements’ positions shall change slightly, reflecting subtle rendering shifts.
steps: Observe the position of elements again.
expected_result: Elements' positions change slightly after reload.

description: For examples listed on the home page but not fully inspected (e.g., 'Slow Resources', 'Secure File Download'), the page shall render their headings and descriptive text, and demonstrate the stated concept.
expected_result: Each page renders its heading and descriptive text, and demonstrates its stated concept (e.g., slow-loading resources, secure file downloads).

description: Due to randomness, a typo is not guaranteed on every page load, and tests should accommodate this non-deterministic behavior.
expected_result: A typo may or may not appear on any given page load.

description: Automation relying on exact text matches for the typo content may intermittently fail due to the intentional randomness.
steps: Attempt to assert an exact text match for the potentially typo-affected content.
expected_result: The assertion for exact text match intermittently fails when a typo is present.

description: Text and images on the dynamic content page may change on every refresh, so tests must not assert exact content unless `?with_content=static` is present in the URL.
expected_result: Text and images change on each refresh.

description: Different notification messages can appear after each 'Click here to load a new message', meaning a test asserting any one specific string may fail intermittently.
steps: Click 'Click here to load a new message' multiple times.
expected_result: Different notification messages appear.

description: Certain menu items may be absent on a given page load, requiring tests relying on their presence to use repeated refresh or conditional logic.
steps: Observe the presence or absence of specific menu items.
expected_result: Specific menu items are sometimes present and sometimes absent.

description: Providing a wrong username, wrong password, or both shall result in an error message and refusal of access to the secure area.
expected_result: An error message is displayed for each invalid attempt, and access to the secure area is refused.

description: Submitting empty username or password fields shall be treated as incorrect information and produce an error message.
steps: Leave Username field empty, enter 'SuperSecretPassword!'. Click Login.
Enter 'tomsmith', leave Password field empty. Click Login.
Leave both fields empty. Click Login.
expected_result: An error message is displayed for each attempt with empty fields, and access to the secure area is refused.

description: When the email field is left blank or an invalid email format is entered, the application may accept and process without specific UI error messages, relying on browser default behavior or backend handling.
steps: Leave the 'E-mail' field blank. Click 'Retrieve password'.
Enter 'invalid-email' in the 'E-mail' field. Click 'Retrieve password'.
expected_result: No explicit UI error message is displayed for blank or invalid email format, and the form submission is processed (or browser default validation occurs).

description: Entering non-numeric characters into the numeric input field shall result in browser default behavior (e.g., ignoring characters) without breaking the page.
steps: Type non-numeric characters (e.g., 'abc') into the numeric input field.
expected_result: The browser handles non-numeric input according to its default behavior (e.g., characters are not entered), and the page does not break or display JavaScript errors.

description: Attempting to submit the file upload form without choosing a file may result in browser-level prohibition or default error, but the application itself does not specify custom error messages.
steps: Click the 'Upload' button without selecting a file.
expected_result: The browser may prevent submission or display a default error message (e.g., 'Please select a file'), and no custom application-specific error message is displayed.

description: Uploading large files or unsupported formats should not crash the page, though specific behavior is not constrained and may vary.
steps: Attempt to upload a very large file (e.g., >1GB).
Attempt to upload a file with an unusual or unsupported format (e.g., .exe).
expected_result: The page does not crash or display critical errors, and behavior for large/unsupported files may vary (e.g., upload fails, takes long, browser error).

description: If the user cancels a confirm dialog or dismisses a prompt dialog without entry, the 'Result:' output should still render without script errors.
steps: Click 'Click for JS Confirm' and then click 'Cancel' in the dialog.
Click 'Click for JS Prompt' and then click 'Cancel' in the dialog.
Observe the 'Result:' area and browser console.
expected_result: The 'Result:' area updates (e.g., 'You clicked: Cancel'), and no JavaScript errors occur.

description: Multiple rapid clicks on alert buttons should result in repeated dialogs without breaking the page.
steps: Rapidly click 'Click for JS Alert' multiple times.
Observe dialog behavior and browser console.
expected_result: Multiple alert dialogs appear sequentially, and the page does not break or display JavaScript errors.

description: Right-clicking outside the specified box area shall present only the default browser context menu, not the custom 'the-internet' item.
steps: Right-click on an area of the page outside the custom context menu box.
expected_result: Only the default browser context menu is displayed, and the 'the-internet' custom item is not present.

description: If the user ignores the alert triggered by context menu selection, the rest of the page should remain functional after the alert is dismissed.
steps: Right-click the box and select 'the-internet' to trigger the alert.
Attempt to interact with other elements on the page (if any).
expected_result: The page remains functional after the alert is dismissed.

description: Hover events on mobile/touch environments may not behave as on desktop, potentially requiring a tap to reveal details, and the primary test focus is desktop browsers.
steps: Observe if hidden details are revealed.
expected_result: Tapping on an image reveals the hidden details (if implemented for touch).

description: Both keyboard and mouse interactions shall be supported for the slider, and repeated movement beyond the minimum or maximum extremes should clamp the value at those limits.
steps: Move the slider to its minimum value using the mouse.
Attempt to move it further left with the keyboard left arrow.
Move the slider to its maximum value using the mouse.
Attempt to move it further right with the keyboard right arrow.
expected_result: The slider value does not go below the minimum or above the maximum.

description: Despite potentially slow rendering or heavy scrolling due to the large DOM, the page shall remain responsive and not produce critical JavaScript errors.
steps: Scroll rapidly up and down the page.
Observe browser responsiveness and developer console for errors.
expected_result: The page remains responsive during scrolling, and no critical JavaScript errors occur.

description: Scrolling beyond the available dynamically loaded content shall gracefully stop adding new sections without error.
steps: Scroll to the very bottom of the page, ensuring all content is loaded.
Observe for new content and errors.
expected_result: New sections stop being added, and no errors are displayed.

description: Very fast scrolling shall not detach the floating menu from its expected position or cause flickering.
steps: Scroll very rapidly up and down the page.
Observe the floating menu's position and stability.
expected_result: The floating menu remains in its expected position relative to the viewport, and no flickering or visual glitches occur.

description: Pixel-level shifts of elements on each page load can cause automation based on absolute positions to fail, highlighting the need for robust locators.
steps: Reload the page and observe positions again.
expected_result: Element positions shift slightly on each load.

description: If the user denies location permission, the page shall handle this gracefully (e.g., by displaying 'no coordinates available') without crashing.
steps: When prompted by the browser, deny location permission.
Observe the page content and browser console.
expected_result: The page displays a message indicating location is unavailable (e.g., 'no coordinates available'), and the page does not crash or display errors.

description: If location services are temporarily unavailable, a message or empty coordinates should appear rather than unhandled errors.
steps: (Simulate location services unavailability, e.g., disable GPS/location in OS settings).
Observe the page content and browser console.
expected_result: A message indicating unavailability or empty coordinates appears, and no unhandled errors are displayed.

description: If pop-up blockers are enabled, the new window may not open, but the base page should still function without errors.
steps: Enable pop-up blockers in the browser.
Observe if a new window opens and the base page's functionality.
expected_result: The new window may not open, but the base /windows page remains functional and displays no errors.

description: The intentional JavaScript error on load should be verifiable, and the page should remain at least partially accessible for inspection despite the error.
steps: Attempt to interact with any visible elements on the page.
expected_result: A JavaScript error is present in the console, and the page content is still visible and at least partially inspectable.

description: Page structures shall be simple and minimalistic, making intended test interactions (clicks, key presses, hovers) easily discoverable without prior instruction.
steps: Observe the layout and controls without prior knowledge.
expected_result: Interactive elements are clearly visible and their purpose is intuitive, and the page layout is uncluttered and easy to understand.

description: Each example page shall include explanatory text at or near the top that clearly states the behavior being demonstrated.
steps: Locate and read the text at the top of the page.
expected_result: Explanatory text is present at or near the top of the page and clearly describes the page's demonstrated behavior.

description: Important interactive controls (e.g., 'Login', 'Add Element', 'Where am I?', 'Click for JS Alert') shall be visually distinguishable as buttons or links and labeled with descriptive text reflecting their actions.
steps: Navigate to pages with interactive controls (e.g., /login, /add_remove_elements/, /geolocation, /javascript_alerts).
Observe the appearance and labels of interactive elements.
expected_result: Interactive controls are clearly identifiable as buttons or links, and their labels accurately describe their actions.

description: The visual appearance of the site shall remain intentionally simple and unobtrusive, allowing users to focus on testing behavior rather than elaborate design details.
steps: Navigate through various pages of the site.
Observe the overall design and styling.
expected_result: The site has a simple, minimalistic, and unobtrusive design with no elaborate or distracting design elements.

description: The site shall provide a consistent and intuitive feel across examples, enabling users to understand new examples quickly based on prior experience.
steps: Observe common UI patterns and navigation.
expected_result: A consistent layout and interaction pattern is maintained across different example pages, making new examples quick to understand.

description: Under normal network conditions, each example page shall render basic static content within a reasonable time frame suitable for interactive testing.
steps: Measure the time taken for the basic static content to render (e.g., using browser dev tools).
expected_result: Basic static content on pages renders within a reasonable time (e.g., < 2-3 seconds).

description: For 'Slow Resources' features, the main page shell shall load in a way that allows testers to perceive and experiment with the intentional delay of certain elements.
steps: Observe the page loading process and the appearance of delayed elements.
expected_result: The main page shell loads relatively quickly, and the delay in loading specific 'slow resources' is observable.

description: Dynamic behaviors relying on page refresh (e.g., Dynamic Content, Typos, Disappearing Elements) shall complete new content rendering immediately after the browser completes the reload event.
steps: Observe the time taken for new content to render after the browser's reload indicator finishes.
expected_result: New content renders immediately after the browser completes the reload event.

description: All example pages listed on the home page shall be accessible via their respective links without resulting in HTTP errors (e.g., 404), unless purposely demonstrating error conditions.
steps: Click each link in the 'Available Examples' section.
Verify each page loads successfully (HTTP 200 OK), except for /status_codes which should load its content as expected.
expected_result: Each linked page loads successfully (HTTP 200 OK), and no HTTP 404 or other client/server errors occur, except for pages intentionally demonstrating error codes.

description: Randomized behaviors (e.g., Typos, Dynamic Content, Notification Messages) shall not cause JavaScript errors that prevent the page from rendering correctly.
steps: Interact with dynamic elements (e.g., click 'load new message').
Monitor the browser console for JavaScript errors.
expected_result: No JavaScript errors are reported in the console, and the page renders correctly despite randomized content.

description: The site shall tolerate repeated navigation, refresh, and interaction cycles without requiring a restart or leading to inconsistent, unrecoverable states.
steps: Navigate between several example pages repeatedly.
Perform interactions (clicks, inputs) repeatedly on various pages.
Observe for unexpected behavior, crashes, or unrecoverable states.
expected_result: The site remains stable and functional throughout repeated actions, with no crashes, freezes, or unrecoverable states.

description: The site shall operate correctly on common modern desktop browsers that support JavaScript, HTML5 forms, and the Geolocation API.
steps: Test the site on different modern desktop browsers (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Edge).
Verify core functionalities (e.g., forms, geolocation) work as expected on each browser.
expected_result: All features function correctly across specified modern browsers.

description: Context-menu, alert, confirm, and prompt examples shall behave consistently with browser-native UI patterns for those dialogs, without requiring additional plugins.
steps: Trigger alerts, confirms, prompts, and the custom context menu.
Observe the appearance and behavior of the dialogs.
expected_result: Dialogs appear and behave as native browser dialogs, and no additional plugins are required for dialog functionality.

description: Multiple windows and tab behaviors (e.g., /windows, /multiple_windows) shall function correctly in standard desktop browsers, opening new windows or tabs as configured by browser settings.
steps: Click links designed to open new windows/tabs.
Verify new windows/tabs open according to browser settings.
expected_result: New windows or tabs open as expected, and the functionality works correctly across standard desktop browsers.

description: Each example page shall be logically independent, ensuring that changes to one example do not affect the functionality of others.
steps: Navigate to /login and successfully log in.
Navigate to /checkboxes and interact with checkboxes.
Navigate back to /login and verify login state is as expected (e.g., still logged in or session managed independently).
Navigate to /checkboxes again and verify its state is independent of login.
expected_result: The state and functionality of one example page are not affected by interactions on another example page.

description: Example pages shall be addressable directly by URL, allowing them to be used independently as test fixtures or training assets.
steps: Directly navigate to various example page URLs (e.g., type '/checkboxes' into the browser address bar).
Verify the page loads correctly without requiring prior navigation from the home page.
expected_result: Each example page loads correctly when accessed directly by its URL, without requiring prior navigation from the home page.

description: The overall aesthetic of the application should feel clean and uncluttered, with ample whitespace and minimal distractions, allowing users to concentrate on the functional behavior under test.
steps: Visually inspect various pages of the application.
Assess the layout, spacing, and presence of distracting elements.
expected_result: Pages appear clean and uncluttered, with ample whitespace and minimal visual distractions, facilitating focus on testing.

description: The typography and spacing across pages should provide a sense of consistency and calmness, avoiding abrupt changes in font size or layout that might confuse or distract users during testing activities.
steps: Visually inspect typography (font size, family) and spacing across different pages.
Look for abrupt changes or inconsistencies.
expected_result: Typography and spacing are consistent across pages, contributing to a calm visual experience without abrupt changes in font size or layout.

description: Color usage should remain subtle and neutral, conveying a professional demo environment, while still making controls and headings easy to visually scan.
steps: Visually inspect the color palette used across the site.
Assess the visibility of controls and headings.
expected_result: Colors are subtle and neutral, conveying a professional demo environment, and controls and headings are still easily scannable.
